     The Numbers Business Report

Boyhood (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $25,379,975Details
International Box Office $31,893,074
Worldwide Box Office $57,273,049
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,796,282
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,527,758
Total $6,324,040
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Filmed over 12 years with the same cast, Boyhood is a story of growing up as seen through the eyes of a child named Mason, who literally grows up on screen before our eyes. It charts the rocky terrain of childhood, snapshots of adolescence from road trips and family dinners to birthdays and graduations and all the moments in between. It is both a nostalgic time capsule of the recent past and an ode to growing up and parenting.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$387,618 (1.5% of total gross)
Legs:10.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:44.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$4,000,000 (worldwide box office is 14.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/775 max. theaters, 8.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,102,527

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 11th, 2014 (Limited) by IFC Films
International Releases: June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Austria)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Switzerland)
July 11th, 2014 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 16th, 2014 (Wide) (Belgium)
Video Release: January 6th, 2015 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language including sexual references, and for teen drug and alcohol use.
(Rating bulletin 2314, 3/19/2014)
Running Time: 164 minutes
Award nomination: 2015 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama
Cast, crew, or production detail: Unnamed Character
Plot point: Bullies, Children Dealing with Divorce, Coming of Age, Dysfunctional Family, Relationships Gone Wrong, Road Trip
Social setting: Camping
Subgenre: Coming-of-Age Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Detour Filmproduction
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Filmed over 12 years with the same cast, Boyhood is a story of growing up as seen through the eyes of a child named Mason, who literally grows up on screen before our eyes. It charts the rocky terrain of childhood, snapshots of adolescence from road trips and family dinners to birthdays and graduations and all the moments in between. It is both a nostalgic time capsule of the recent past and an ode to growing up and parenting.
